subject: English
chapter: Nelson Mandela – Long Walk to Freedom

activities_projects.title: Freedom Word Cloud
activities_projects.objective: Understand the meaning of freedom through simple expression.
steps: Write the word ‘Freedom’ in the center
Add words related to freedom (equality, rights, justice, courage)
Decorate the chart neatly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand the concept of freedom and connect it with the chapter.
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevance of words and neat presentation

activities_projects.title: Mandela Timeline
activities_projects.objective: Learn important events in Nelson Mandela’s life.
steps: Draw a simple timeline
Mark important events like imprisonment and becoming President
Add short descriptions for each event
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will recall major events of Mandela’s life.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sequence of events and clarity

activities_projects.title: Inauguration Day Drawing
steps: Draw the scene of Mandela’s swearing-in ceremony
Label key elements like national flag and leaders
Write one sentence about its importance
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand the significance of the historic event.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to the chapter

activities_projects.title: Character Traits Chart
steps: Write Mandela’s name in the center
List at least five qualities (brave, determined, forgiving)
Support each quality with one example from the text
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will describe character traits with textual support.

activities_projects.title: New Vocabulary List
steps: Find 10 new words from the lesson
Write meanings
Use each word in a simple sentence
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will enhance vocabulary and sentence formation.

activities_projects.title: Short Speech on Freedom
steps: Prepare a short 1-minute speech on ‘What Freedom Means to Me’
Practice at home
Present in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will gain confidence in public speaking.

activities_projects.title: Freedom vs Slavery Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the difference between freedom and oppression.
steps: Draw two columns titled ‘Freedom’ and ‘Slavery’
Write examples from the chapter under each
Discuss findings in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will clearly understand contrasting ideas.

activities_projects.title: Quote Poster Making
steps: Choose an inspiring quote from the chapter
Write it neatly on chart paper
Add simple illustrations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will appreciate inspirational values from the lesson.

activities_projects.title: Role Play – Inauguration Speech
activities_projects.objective: Experience the importance of Mandela’s speech.
steps: Select a part of the inauguration speech
Practice reading with expression
Perform in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand tone and emotion in speeches.

activities_projects.title: Message of the Chapter Poster
activities_projects.objective: Identify the central message of the lesson.
steps: Write the main message of the chapter
Add 2–3 supporting points
Present your poster briefly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students will understand the core idea of equality and unity.
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of message and presentation skills
